tests.name: baseline_phase2_feature_recorded
tests.details: Built sponsored_poc baseline: --start-feature uat-feat-1, all 6 build_loop steps completed (tests_written, tests_verified_failing, implemented, security_audit [stub audit md created], documentation_updated, feature_recorded). git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded with PreToolUse gate inactive in shell context. test-gate.sh --record-feature 'uat-feat-1' returned RC=0; build-progress.json features_completed contains uat-feat-1.

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production exits 1. Core upgrade succeeded (Upgrade Complete banner printed, phase-state.json poc_mode removed, last_upgrade=2026-04-25 written, audit row appended, commit made), and post-upgrade verify-install.sh ran cleanly. BUT the BL-009/BL-015 helper-refresh block at line 1471 calls `cp $SCRIPT_DIR/$helper scripts/$helper`. When the user invokes the project's local copy of upgrade-project.sh (the documented invocation), $SCRIPT_DIR resolves to $PROJECT_DIR/scripts, so source==dest. macOS BSD cp exits 1 on identical files ('cp: scripts/pending-approval.sh and .../scripts/pending-approval.sh are identical (not copied).'). With set -euo pipefail at the top of upgrade-project.sh, the script aborts, the final 'Upgrade complete.' line never prints, and RC=1. Reproduced deterministically. U-E (poc_mode lookup) appears NOT regressed — the script DID successfully read poc_mode=sponsored_poc from phase-state.json before aborting.

tests.name: phase_state_preserved
tests.details: .project=uat-agent-81 unchanged; .current_phase=0 unchanged; .track=full unchanged; .deployment=organizational unchanged. .poc_mode key removed (correct for production target). .last_upgrade=2026-04-25 added.

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: .build_loop reset (feature=null, steps_completed=[]) — that's the post-feature_recorded state set BEFORE the upgrade ran, not an upgrade-side mutation. build-progress.json features_completed still contains uat-feat-1; features_since_last_test=1. Existing feature history preserved.

Post-upgrade verify-install.sh enumerates required structure (CLAUDE.md, PROJECT_INTAKE.md, governance-framework.md, etc.) all present.

PR #17 U-F fix stays effective.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh aborts at end via cp identical-file under set -euo pipefail
framework_bugs.reproduction: Build any project via init.sh --non-interactive (so scripts/pending-approval.sh and scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh are copied into the project). cd into the project. Run `bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production` (or any upgrade path that hits the BL-009/BL-015 refresh block at scripts/upgrade-project.sh line 1469). Script reaches '[STEP] Refreshing framework helper scripts (BL-009, BL-015)', then runs `cp $SCRIPT_DIR/pending-approval.sh scripts/pending-approval.sh` where $SCRIPT_DIR resolves to $PROJECT_DIR/scripts (because the user invokes the project's local copy of the script — the documented invocation per Setup Complete next-steps banner and per the upgrade-project.sh --help). cp exits 1 ('are identical (not copied)'); set -euo pipefail aborts the script.
framework_bugs.expected: Helper refresh either skips no-op same-file copies, or uses `cp -f` with `|| true`, or compares paths first. Final 'Upgrade complete.' banner prints. RC=0.
framework_bugs.actual: Script terminates immediately after `cp: ... are identical (not copied).`. RC=1. The closing 'Upgrade complete.' line and the print_step 'Running post-upgrade validation' block (validate.sh) at the bottom of the script never execute. The earlier portion of the upgrade DID complete (state files written, audit logged, commit made, 'Upgrade Complete' banner from middle-of-script printed, post-upgrade verify-install ran), so the project state is technically correct — but exit-code-checking automation will treat the upgrade as failed. Affects every fresh-init upgrade on macOS. Suggested fix: replace the cp loop with `if ! cmp -s "$SCRIPT_DIR/$helper" "scripts/$helper"; then cp ...; fi` or `cp -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/$helper" "scripts/$helper.tmp" && mv "scripts/$helper.tmp" "scripts/$helper"` or guard with `[[ "$SCRIPT_DIR/$helper" -ef "scripts/$helper" ]] || cp ...`.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: Tracked-file scan for password|api[_-]?key|secret|token across project: hits are all in evaluation-prompts/Projects/bases/03-security.md template wording (e.g. 'Are there any hardcoded secrets, API keys, tokens') — legitimate doc content, no actual credentials.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: .claude/process-audit.log absent (no FORCE/reset events occurred during this run). N/A per runbook guidance.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only pre-existing untracked '.claude/' and 'Reports/uat-2026-04-26/' (from sweep setup, not this agent). git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD = d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494, matches the SHA captured at agent setup. Framework-self guard from PR #18 holds.

summary: Init non-interactive (PR #19), framework-self guard (PR #18), U-H helper copy fix (PR #17), and U-F sentinel respect on upgrade (PR #17) all stay fixed. NEW Critical regression: upgrade-project.sh aborts (RC=1) at the BL-009/BL-015 helper-refresh block due to `cp` identical-file failure under set -euo pipefail when invoked from the project's local copy — the documented invocation.
